description:
Recurring bug: when a polecat is dispatched to fix work on an existing PR branch, it creates a new branch from main instead of pushing commits onto the target branch specified via --base-branch.

Observed 3 times in one session:
- rust/hq-uc20: should have pushed to polecat/guzzle/hq-gmn5@mmdwlvlm (PR #2585), created separate branch instead → had to open new PR #2628
- guzzle/mo-qxjr: should have pushed to polecat/chrome/hq-ge28@mmegs623 (PR #2613), created polecat/guzzle/mo-qxjr@mmf79ksi instead → Mayor had to manually fix
- dust/mo-6m9d: should have pushed to polecat/rust/hq-53aw@mmegs5r3 (PR #2612), created polecat/dust/mo-6m9d@mmf81ax6 instead → Mayor had to force-push

Each time, the Mayor had to manually force-push the polecat's branch onto the target branch. The workaround is disruptive and error-prone.

Root cause hypothesis: mol-polecat-work formula does not pass --base-branch (or equivalent) when creating the polecat worktree, or the polecat ignores the branch context in its hook.
title: --base-branch flag ignored: polecats create new branches instead of pushing to specified PR branch
